Prudential ( (GB:PRU) ) just unveiled an update.

Prudential plc has repurchased 251,388 of its ordinary shares of 5 pence each from JP Morgan Securities plc under an existing share buyback arrangement, at prices ranging between £11.0950 and £11.2950 and an average price of £11.1574. The company plans to cancel the repurchased shares, reducing the number of shares and voting rights in issue to 2,525,338,119, a move that tightens its capital base and may support earnings per share and shareholder value while aligning with regulatory requirements across its London and Hong Kong listings.

The shares were bought as on-exchange transactions on the London Stock Exchange and treated as on-market purchases under the Hong Kong Code on Share Buy-Backs, reflecting Prudential’s compliance with multi-jurisdictional market rules. The updated share count also provides a new reference point for investors assessing disclosure thresholds under UK transparency regulations, which may influence institutional reporting and ownership monitoring.

More about Prudential

The company provides life and health insurance, retirement and investment products, with a strong presence in Asia and other growth markets, serving retail and institutional customers seeking protection and wealth management solutions.

China Everbright Water ( (SG:U9E) ) just unveiled an update.

China Everbright Water Limited has announced the payment of interest on its 2023 first tranche medium term notes, which have a principal amount of RMB1 billion and were issued to institutional investors in the Chinese Mainland’s national inter-bank bond market. The notes, known as 23 MTN001, form part of the company’s financing structure and reflect its continued access to domestic debt capital.

The company will pay simple interest at an annual rate of 3.20% for the period from 17 April 2025 to 16 April 2026, with payment scheduled for 17 April 2026 or the next working day if that date falls on a public holiday in the Chinese Mainland. This interest payment, together with previously announced resale arrangements, signals orderly management of its bond obligations and should provide clarity and predictability for noteholders and other stakeholders regarding its near-term funding commitments.

PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation has signed a strategic cooperative framework agreement with the Graduate School of AI Operating System of Shanghai Jiao Tong University to deepen collaboration in intelligent automobile technologies and embodied intelligence based on the OpenHarmony operating system. The partnership aims to combine PATEO’s industry position with SJTU’s research strength to accelerate innovation and build a self-reliant ecosystem for embodied intelligence.

Under the agreement, the parties will jointly pursue advanced academic research, co-host OpenHarmony embodied intelligence forums and seminars, and cultivate ecosystem-focused talent through special projects and open funds. data.title: SSY Group Wins NMPA Approvals for New Electrolyte and Antiemetic Injections
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SSY Group ( (HK:2005) ) has provided an update.

SSY Group Limited has received approvals from China’s National Medical Products Administration for the production and registration of its Multiple Electrolytes, Sodium Acetate and Glucose Injection in 250ml and 500ml formats, as well as its Metoclopramide Hydrochloride Injection. Both products are classified as type 3 chemical drugs and have passed the country’s consistency evaluation, supporting their competitiveness in the regulated hospital market.

The electrolyte and glucose injection is indicated for replacing and maintaining water and electrolyte balance while providing energy when oral intake is not feasible, reinforcing SSY Group’s core infusion therapy portfolio. The Metoclopramide injection, used to prevent and treat various causes of nausea and vomiting and to assist certain diagnostic procedures, builds on the group’s earlier approval for its Metoclopramide bulk drug and is expected to broaden its injectable offerings and strengthen its position in China’s clinical treatment segment.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited ( (HK:1442) ) has issued an update.

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited reported audited consolidated results for the year ended 31 December 2025, showing a slight decline in revenue to RM452.7 million from RM475.8 million and a modest drop in gross profit, reflecting a softer operating environment in its logistics and transport business. The group’s performance was further pressured by a sharp increase in loss allowances for receivables and higher administrative expenses, which, together with finance costs and tax charges, swung the company from a prior-year profit to a net loss attributable to shareholders, signaling near-term challenges for profitability and potentially heightened risk awareness among investors and other stakeholders.

Total comprehensive income also turned negative, weighed down by foreign exchange translation losses, underscoring the group’s exposure to currency movements across its operating markets. Basic and diluted earnings per share moved from positive territory to a small loss, highlighting the deterioration in shareholder returns and suggesting that management may face greater scrutiny over capital allocation, credit control, and cost management as the company seeks to stabilize results and protect its market position in the logistics sector.

data.title: Manulife Sets Hybrid 2026 Annual Meetings for Shareholders and Policyholders
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Manulife Financial ( (TSE:MFC) ) has provided an update.

Manulife Financial Corporation has scheduled its 2026 annual meeting of common shareholders, alongside the annual meeting of policyholders and the shareholder of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company, for May 14, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern time in Toronto with a concurrent live webcast. The hybrid format enables shareholders, policyholders, and duly appointed proxyholders to ask questions and vote electronically or in person, underscoring the company’s continued emphasis on accessibility and engagement in its governance process.

Investors and policyholders are being strongly encouraged to vote and submit proxies ahead of the meeting date, with detailed instructions and materials available on Manulife’s annual meeting website. Policyholders of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company are also provided with a formal channel to request notice of future meetings, reinforcing transparency and ongoing communication between the insurer and its stakeholders.

More about Manulife Financial

Manulife Financial Corporation is a global financial services group that provides insurance, wealth management, and asset management solutions to individual and institutional clients. Through its primary insurance subsidiary, The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company, Manulife focuses on life insurance, retirement, and investment products across key markets including Canada and internationally.

data.title: Mexan’s Controlling Shareholder Sells Entire Stake, New Substantial Investors Emerge
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mexan ( (HK:0022) ) has provided an update.

Mexan Limited has announced the completion of a major share placing by its controlling shareholder, referred to as the Vendor, involving 1,326,735,354 existing shares, or about 67.47% of the company’s issued share capital. These shares were placed by a placing agent to at least six independent investors, each restricted from holding 30% or more of the company after the transaction, in line with Hong Kong takeover regulations.

Following completion of the placing on 9 April 2026, the Vendor ceased to hold any shares and is no longer the controlling shareholder of Mexan, marking a significant change in the company’s ownership structure. Two of the placees, investor Chen Jixiang and Max Premier Limited, which he ultimately owns, together acquired around 17.64% of Mexan’s shares and have become substantial shareholders, prompting the company to advise existing shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its stock.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0022) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.25 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mexan stock, see the HK:0022 Stock Forecast page.

More about Mexan

Mexan Limited, listed in Hong Kong under stock code 22, operates as a publicly traded company with a diverse shareholder base and a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors. The firm is overseen by chairman Lun Yiu Kay Edwin, with corporate governance structured in line with Hong Kong listing rules and market regulations.

data.title: Sino Biopharm Advances Kylo-11 With First U.S. Patient in Phase II Trial
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sino Biopharmaceutical ( (HK:1177) ) has provided an update.

Sino Biopharmaceutical announced that its subsidiary Hangzhou Hygieia Biomedical has enrolled the first U.S. patient in a Phase II clinical trial of Kylo-11, an siRNA drug targeting the LPA gene for patients with atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and elevated lipoprotein(a). The study, conducted in both China and the United States, will test dosing regimens of once every six months or once a year, positioning the therapy as a potential best-in-class, ultra-long-acting treatment for a major, currently unaddressed cardiovascular risk factor.

Kylo-11 uses a dual-conjugated siRNA delivery platform that has received global patent authorization and aims to deliver durable reductions in Lp(a) levels with favorable safety and tolerability. Early Phase I data showed robust and sustained lowering of Lp(a) across multiple dose cohorts with mostly mild, unrelated adverse events, underscoring the drug’s potential to fill a global therapeutic gap and strengthen Sino Biopharmaceutical’s presence in innovative cardiovascular therapies.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1177) stock is a Buy with a HK$8.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sino Biopharmaceutical stock, see the HK:1177 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sino Biopharmaceutical

Sino Biopharmaceutical Limited is a China-based pharmaceutical group engaged in the research, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of innovative medicines. Through its wholly owned subsidiary Hangzhou Hygieia Biomedical, the company focuses on cutting-edge RNA interference therapies, targeting major unmet needs in cardiovascular and other chronic diseases in global markets.
